mvw.mvw_command: cargo build --release --bin nu && for i in $(seq 1 50); do ./target/release/nu -c 'open mvw_input/sample.json | each {|x| \$x.b | math sum } | math sum'; done
mvw.run_command: for i in $(seq 1 50); do ./target/release/nu -c 'open mvw_input/sample.json | each {|x| \$x.b | math sum } | math sum'; done
mvw.input_scale: local JSON file with 3 records, each containing a short integer array; repeated 50 invocations
mvw.success_criteria: both commands exit code 0 and target/release/nu exists

environment.methodology: tutorial.md one-pass profiling; process-tree RSS peak (monitor_tree.sh), whole tree not single process; MVW actually run; multi-thread/multi-process peak included.

static_triage.notes: Rust shell project; no GPU requirement; build can be CPU/RAM heavy due to Rust dependency compilation.

peaks_observed.cpu_utilization_summary: build high (cargo/rustc compile), run low-moderate (single-process CLI data pipeline)

min_hardware.summary: 2 vCPU / 16 GB RAM / 2 GB disk, no GPU. Raw process-tree peaks: build 9.0417 GB, run 0.0072 GB, disk 1.5354 GB.
